properties.remark: MUD AND SMALL DEBRIS FLOWING DOWN WILLIAMS AND SANTIAGO CANYON DUE TO MODERATE RAIN OVER THE BOND FIRE BURN SCAR. MUD COVERING PARTS OF SANTIAGO CANYON RD.
MUD SLIDE COVERING HALF OF SILVERADO CANYON RD IN THE AREA OF THE BOND FIRE BURN SCAR. HEAVY RAIN IMPACTED THE AREA, WITH RATES UP TO 0.40 INCHES PER HOUR AROUND THE TIME OF THE REPORT.
SILVERADO CANYON AT OLIVE DRIVE. ROADS ARE IMPASSIBLE FROM MULTIPLE MUDFLOWS. BULLDOZERS ON SCENE TRYING TO CLEAR IT.

ORANGE COUNTY PUBLIC WORKS REPORTS MODERATE DEBRIS FLOW IN SILVERADO CANYON ON BOND FIRE SCAR. MUD AND GRAVEL AS HIGH AS 4 FEET BY HOME GARAGE AND BURYING LAWN FURNITURE NEAR HOME AND SMALL BOULDERS. MUD ON ROADWAYS. CURRENTLY CREWS STILL WORKING ON REMOVAL. PHOTOS AVAILABLE ON TWITTER. RAINFALL RATES OF 0.40 TO 0.50 INCH PER HOUR OBSERVED 10 PM THURDAY TO MIDNIGHT.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 2 NW LAKE FOREST. MUDSLIDE BLOCKING LANE I-5N/I405. ROCKS AND DIRT IN ROAD.
MUDSLIDE BLOCKING LANE I-5N/I405. ROCKS AND DIRT IN ROAD.
LARGE BOULDERS IN THE ROAD.
LARGE BOULDER BLOCKING LANES ON SR 79 AT PERALTA DR IN HOLCOMB VILLAGE PER CHP.
CHP REPORTS MUDSLIDE ON HIGHWAY 74 ORTEGA HIGHWAY. 0.50 INCH RAINFALL PAST 6 HOURS AND 1.5 INCHES PAST 24 HOURS AT 2 GAUGES.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS DUST STORM REPORT FROM 5 NNW PALM SPRINGS. PALM SPRINGS PD REPORTED N INDIAN CYN DR WAS CLOSED BETWEEN GARNET AND SUNRISE PKWY DUE TO DRIFTING SAND ON THE ROADWAY AND NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY. ROAD REOPENED AROUND 7 AM SUNDAY.
PALM SPRINGS PD REPORTED N INDIAN CYN DR WAS CLOSED BETWEEN GARNET AND SUNRISE PKWY DUE TO DRIFTING SAND CAUSING VISIBILITY TO REDUCE TO NEAR ZERO.

UPGRADE FROM DEBRIS FLOW REPORT. 2 VIDEOS OF FLASH FLOOD MUD AND DEBRIS WATERS LEAVING ANDERSON CREEK BETWEEN HOMES AND MOVING VEHICLES. IN SILVERADO TOWN OFF SILVERADO CANYON ROAD. 2 VEHICLES PUSHED INTO EACH OTHER AND ANOTHER PUSHED INTO CREEK BED. MAJOR MUDDY WATER FLOW OVER 6 INCHES FLOWING DOWN STREET. FIRE DEPARTMENT RESCUED HOMEOWNERS TRAPPED. NO REPORTED INJURY. WATER AND DEBRIS SURROUNDED 2 HOMES. NEAR SILVERADO CAFé. RAINFALL RATE OBSERVED LOWER DOWN THE CANYON AT 0.18 IN 15 MINUTES AND TOTAL 0.55 INCHES ON THE BOND FIRE BURN SCAR.
CHP REPORTS SILVERADO ROAD CLOSED AT CAFé DUE TO MUD SLIDE. RAINFALL UP TO 0.25 PER HOUR IN THIS AREA PAST 2 HOURS. THIS IS THE BOND FIRE SCAR.

PUBLIC WORKS AND ORANGE COUNTY FIRE ON SITE WORKING TO REMOVE 2 VEHICLES STUCK IN MUD AND WATER ALONG ANDERSON CREEK. AREA RECEIVED HEAVY RAINFALL OF 0.55 INCHES PAST 3 HOURS ACROSS BOND FIRE SCAR. ROAD IS CLOSED FOR ACTIVITY.

MUD AND GRAVEL ON SILVERADO ROAD WITH ONE SLIDE UP TO 150 CUBIC YARDS. 4 LOCATIONS WITH MUD FLOW. DEPTH ENOUGH TO CAUSE AT LEAST 2 VEHICLESTO BE STUCK IN MUD ON MAIN ROAD. 1 LOCATION HAS PUSHED 2 VEHICLES INTO HOUSE NEAR SILVERADO CAFé. ANOTHER UNKNOWN SLIDE IN WILLIAMS CANYON. NO INJURIES REPORTED. THERE ARE EVACUATION ORDERS NOW FOR SILVERADO CANYON AND EVACUATION WARNING FOR MODJESKA CANYON. BOND FIRE SCAR LOCATION BETWEEN THESE 2 CANYONS. RAINFALL WAS BETWEEN 4 AND 7 AM PST WITH 0.40 TO 0.55 INCHES TOTAL ON 3 GAUGES AND HOURLY RATES UP TO 0.25 INCH. ORANGE COUNTY UNIFIED COMMAND AT LOCATION IN SILVERADO MANAGING.

LARGE BOULDER UP TO 4 FOOT DIAMETER ON POPPET FLAT ROAD AND HIGHWAY 243 AREA. BUCKET LOADER BROUGHT UP FROM BANNING AS SNOW PLOW COULD NOT MOVE. AREA HAD RAIN BELOW 5500 FEET. 0.25 TO 0.45 INCH RAINFALL IN 6 HOUR PERIOD.
LARGE BOULDER ON ALPINE LANE NEAR HIGHWAY 189 REPORTED BY CALTRANS. AREA HAD A MIXTURE OF RAIN AND SNOW TODAY.
A CONVECTIVE SHOWER PRODUCED A WIND GUST OF 69 MPH. COACHELLA MESONET/HRRCOA. TIME OF OCCURRENCE BETWEEN 4 PM AND 4:30 PM.
CONVECTIVE SHOWER PRODUCED A WIND GUST OF 60 MPH AT THERMAL AIRPORT.
CALTRANS REPORTS LARGE TREE BRANCH BLOCKING MIDDLE LANES OF STATE ROUTE 86 SOUTH.
PUBLIC REPORTED WATER SPOUT NEAR OCEANSIDE HARBOR AROUND 8:25 AM.
INDIAN CANYON DRIVE AND GENE AUTRY TRAIL WERE CLOSED SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 10 DUE TO BLOWING SAND AND RELATED LOW VISIBILITY. NEARBY WEATHER STATION CF142 REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 44 MPH AT 615 PM.
KESQ PALM SPRINGS REPORTS ROAD CLOSURE OF NORTH INDIAN CANYON DRIVE DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
CALTRANS AND MEDIA REPORTING NORTH GENE AUTRY TRAIL IS CLOSED DUE TO BLOWING SAND ACROSS THE ROAD. WINDS ARE GUSTING OUT OF THE WEST AT AROUND 40 MPH (CALTRANS CF142 SENSOR).
CALTRANS AND MEDIA REPORTING NORTH INDIAN CANYON DRIVE IS CLOSED DUE TO BLOWING SAND ACROSS THE ROAD. WINDS ARE GUSTING OUT OF THE WEST AT AROUND 40 MPH (CALTRANS CF142 SENSOR).
A MICROBURST PRODUCED A 60 MPH WIND GUST WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM AROUND 3:15 AM PDT.

DELAYED REPORT. RADAR ESTIMATED 2 TO 2.5 INCHES OF RAINFALL AT THE MOUTH OF BOX CANYON WHICH CAUSED FLASH FLOODING AT THE ENTRANCE OF BOX CANYON ROAD AND ALONG PAINTED CANYON ROAD. THERE WERE A COUPLE VEHICLES INVOLVED WITH THE FLOODING WHICH CAUSED A ROAD CLOSURE. LOCATED SOUTH OF I-10 AND NORTH OF HIGHWAY 111.
ONE VEHICLE SWEPT OFF THE ROADWAY ON BOX CANYON ROAD WITH 10 TO 12 OTHER VEHICLES POTENTIALLY STRANDED.
CALTRANS REPORTS BOULDERS IN THE ROADWAY NEAR VISTA POINT ON SR 74.

DAMAGE AND WASHED OUT SECTION OF CONCRETE SIDEWALK AS EMBANKMENT GAVE WAY TO FLOODING ALONG COUNTRY CLUB DRIVE AND AVENUE 42. ALSO WATER COVERED ROADWAY ON JEFFERSON STREET. PHOTOS SHARED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT. RAIN OF 0.70 PER HOUR OBSERVED AND UP TO 1.60 INCHES MEASURED IN 3 HOURS.
FLOODING ON VARNER ROAD.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 5 SSE BIG BEAR LAKE. CALTRANS REPORTS ROAD CLOSURE BOTH DIRECTIONS ON HIGHWAY 38 FROM GLASS ROAD TO SEVEN OAKS DUE TO ROCK AND MUDSLIDES ON ROADWAY. RESTRICTION FROM FOREST FALLS TO LAKE WILLIAMS DRIVE. RADAR HAS LARGE THUNDERSTORMS OVER HIGHWAY 38 AND UPPER SANTA ANA WATERSHED ESTIMATED 1.5 TO 2.3 INCHES OF RAIN ON RADAR IN FROG CREEK, BARTON CREEK AND SOUTH FORK SANTA ANA RIVER. ROAD STILL CLOSED AS OF 630 PM. LARGE ROCKS, LOGS, MUD AND BOULDERS BEING REMOVED. SOME DAMAGE REPORTED TO ROAD.

CALTRANS AND CHP AT BARTON FLATS REPORT SIGNIFICANT DEBRIS ON HIGHWAY 38. VEHICLES REPORTED STUCK AND ROAD IS IMPASSABLE AT 3 LOCATIONS. MUD, ROCKS AND LOG DEBRIS AND FLOODING HAS DAMAGE PART OF STATE HIGHWAY 38. THE ROAD IS CLOSED AT LEAST FOR 3 HOURS. RADAR ESTIMATED 2 TO 2.5 INCHES IN DRAINAGE OF BARTON CREEK AND SANTA ANA SOUTH FORK. PHOTOS SHARED OF DEBRIS ON ROADWAY.

PER CHP CALTRANS ONSITE. ROAD COVERED UP TO 5 FEET OF MUD, ROCKS, DEBRIS AND LOGS AT JENKS LAKE ROAD AND BOYS CAMP. NO ACCESS TO BIG BEAR ON STATE HIGHWAY 38. AT LEAST 3 LOCATIONS WITH SIGNIFICANT DEBRIS ON ROADWAY. EQUIPMENT CLEARING PATH TO ASSESS DAMAGE TO ROADWAYS.
AUTOMATED WEATHER STATION REPORTING 1.5 INCHES OF RAINFALL PAST 1 HOUR. GARNER VALLEY AT LAKE HEMET.
TWITTER REPORT WITH VIDEO OF HEAVY BLOWING DUST IN BORREGO SPRINGS. CORRESPONDING PEAK WIND WAS 30 MPH FROM THE SSE AT APPROXIMATELY 7 PM PDT. MINIMUM VISIBILITY UNKNOWN. DURATION ESTIMATED.
CHP REPORTED FLASH FLOODING AT BORREGO SALTON SEAWAY (S22) AT FONTS WASH. CAR WAS STUCK IN THE LOW WATER CROSSING.
ROCK SLIDE ON HIGHWAY 371 EAST OF BURNT VALLEY ROAD. ROCKS IN ROADWAY. SEVERAL VEHICLES PARKED ON SIDE OF ROAD.
HUGE TREE DOWNED ON TAMARISK ROW BY STRONG WINDS. REPORTED BY KESQ TV.
FLASH FLOOD ON BORREGO SALTON SEAWAY AT PALO VERDE WASH. TWO CARS STUCK IN WATER AND MUD. MOTORISTS ABANDONED THEIR VEHICLES AND FLED TO HIGHER GROUND. CHP CLOSED THE ROAD.
CHP REPORTED ROCKS IN THE ROAD ON HIGHWAY 78 BETWEEN SAN FELIPE ROAD AND YAQUI PASS ROAD.
HIGHWAY 78 AT MASTROS TRAILER PARK ROAD COVERED WITH MUD AND ROCKS. DEBRIS FLOW IS 100 FT WIDE AND 1 FT DEEP.
REPORTED BRANCHES DOWN ON ROADWAY AND SMALL TREES. SAN VINCENTE ROAD AND GUNN STAGE. WIND GUSTS NEARBY 40 TO 45 MPH ON MESONET.
TREE DOWN AT MUSSEY GRADE RD / MAHOGANY RANCH RD.
CHP REPORT OF ROCK SLIDES ALONG SUNRISE HIGHWAY JUST NORTH OF I-8.

CALTRANS REPORTED DEBRIS FLOW UP TO 2 FEET OFF EL DORADO BURN SCAR. 50 CUBIC YARDS OF MUD AND DEBRIS WERE REMOVED ALONG HIGHWAY 38 NEAR BARTON FLATS RADAR ESTIMATED ABOUT 0.50 INCH IN 1 HOUR FROM THUNDERSTORM.
STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS RESULTED IN ROOF DAMAGE AT THE GALILEE CENTER, USED AS A SHELTER FOR PEOPLE WHO HAD LOST POWER THE DAY BEFORE. THE LEAKING ROOF PROMPTED EVACUATION OF THE BUILDING.
COUPLE TREES WERE DOWNED AT LA QUINTA HIGH SCHOOL BY HIGH WINDS OUTFLOW FROM THUNDERSTORMS TO THE SOUTH. SOUTHEAST WIND GUSTS 40 TO 45 MPH IN THE LOWER COACHELLA VALLEY.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM 1 SSE THOUSAND PALMS. TREE FELL ON VEHICLE. WINDS AT THOUSAND PALMS WERE 26 MPH GUSTING TO 43 MPH AT THE TIME.
HUGE TREE BLOCKING THE STREET.
CHP CALTRANS REPORTING ROCKS SLIDING ONTO HIGHWAY 18 ARCTIC CIRCLE DUE TO THUNDERSTORMS CURRENTLY THERE. THIS IS LOCATED NEAR THE SHARP WINDING PORTION OF THE HIGHWAY.
CHP CLOSURE ON HIGHWAY 18. RADAR RAINFALL OF 1 TO 2 INCHES IN 1 HOUR ON THE STEEP SLOPES AT THE WINDY PORTION OF ARCTIC CIRCLE HIGHWAY 18. ROCK SLIDES REPORTED ONTO HIGHWAY.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 5 W BIG BEAR LAKE. CHP CLOSURE ON HIGHWAY 18. RADAR RAINFALL OF 1 TO 2 INCHES IN 1 HOUR ON THE STEEP SLOPES AT THE WINDING PORTION OF ARCTIC CIRCLE HIGHWAY 18. ROCK SLIDES REPORTED ONTO HIGHWAY.
MESONET STATION NRWSD NARROWS SUB (SDGE). TEMPERATURE DROPPED FROM 101 TO 74F. THIS IS ALONG HIGHWAY 78.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 8 NW WARNER SPRINGS. SPOTTER USED RULER AND QUARTER.
CORRECTS TIME OF PREVIOUS TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM 5 E RAINBOW. TEMPERATURE DROPPED 10 DEGREES. SDGE RANCHO HEIGHTS.
LARGE TREE DOWN AND ON ROADWAY AT OAK VIEW PL AND SCENIC VIEW ROAD. CHAIN SAW REQUIRED.
LARGE TREE ON VEHICLE AT OAK VIEW PLACE AND SCENIC VIEW ROAD.
NBC AND CBS REPORTED LARGE 70 FOOT PINE TREE THAT WAS DOWNED ON A HOME AND DAMAGED A GARAGE AS WELL AS DOWNED A POWER LINE. SMALLER TREE DOWN IN THE SAME AREA. 4500 PANORAMA DRIVE.
CAMPO ROAD FLOODED BETWEEN AVACOADO AND VIA MERCADO. 3 VEHICLES WERE STUCK IN THE WATER.
LARGE ROCK IN ROADWAY HIGHWAY 74 AT CASPERS PARK. THUNDERSTORMS MOVED THROUGH.
TEMPERATURE DROPPED 10 DEGREES. SDGE RANCHO HEIGHTS.
VISIBILITY LESS THAN 1/2 MILE IN BLOWING DUST.
ROCKS ON HIGHWAY 330 LANES AND AT LEAST 1 LARGE BOULDER REPORTED. HEAVY RAINFALL OF 0.5 TO 0.7 IN 1 HOUR OCCURRED IN THIS AREA. LOCATION AT THE HIGHWAY 330 PASSING LANE.
LARGE BOULDER IN LANE ON HIGHWAY 18 AT GREGORY LANE REPORTED ON QUICKMAP CHP CALTRANS. HEAVY RAIN BAND MOVED THROUGH THIS AREA. 0.7 INCH PER HOUR RATE.
MUD REPORTED ON HIGHWAY 18 AT BIG BEAR DAM. BOTH LANES AFFECTED.

ROCKS IN BOTH LANES ON HIGHWAY 18. HEAVY RAIN OF 0.4 TO 0.7 INCHES IN 1 HOUR JUST MOVED THROUGH THIS AREA. CALTRANS AND CHP REPORT ON QUICKMAP. STEEP ROCKY TERRAIN BETWEEN BIG BEAR DAM AND SNOW VALLEY.
CHP AND CALTRANS REPORTS MUDSLIDE, UNKNOWN DEPTH BUT AFFECTING 1 VEHICLE IN MUD ON HIGHWAY 38.
LARGE BOULDER AND OTHER ROCKS IN LANE OF HIGHWAY 38 REPORTED BY CALTRANS AND CHP. HEAVY RAIN IS OCCURRING IN THE AREA WITH RAINFALL RATE OF 0.25 TO 0.50 PAST HOUR. MOUNTAIN HOME VILLAGE.
MUD ON HIGHWAY 38 REPORTED BY HIGHWAY PATROL AND CALTRANS. HEAVY RAIN BAND JUST MOVED THROUGH THIS AREA. THIS IS AT EL DORADO BURN SCAR.
HOSPITAL ROAD AND STATE ROUTE 173 ROADWAY DAMAGE REPORTED FROM HEAVY RAIN. 1.18 INCHES RAINFALL IN 3 HOURS REPORTED NEARBY.
AT LIVE OAK DR LARGE BOULDER IN LANE OF HIGHWAY 330 PER CHP CALTRANS QUICKMAP.
ROAD CLOSURE ON HIGHWAY 38 FROM VALLEY OF THE FALLS TO LAKE WILLIAMS FOR DEBRIS CLEAN UP.

FIELD SURVEY ON OCTOBER 27, SHOWED NUMEROUS SMALL DEBRIS FLOWS FROM EL DORADO FIRE SCAR (2020) WHICH WENT ACROSS HIGHWAY 38 CAUSING A CLOSURE. CALTRANS REMOVED UP TO 1000 CUBIC YARDS OF MUD AND DEBRIS. MILE MARKER 15 TO 19 MOST DEBRIS ABOVE VALLEY OF THE FALLS.

SEVERAL ROCK SLIDES FROM VALLEY OF FALLS TO SUGAR PINE DRIVE ALONG HIGHWAY 38. ROAD CLOSED FOR CLEAN UP AND REMOVAL OF DEBRIS. CALTRANS AND SHERIFF AT LOCATION. HEAVY RAIN OCCURRED IN THIS AREA PAST 2 HOURS.

FIELD SURVEY REVEALED HYPER CONCENTRATED DEBRIS FLOW IN BIRCH CREEK FROM EL DORADO BURN SCAR. UP TO 3 FEET OF WATER CROSS POTATO CANYON ROAD AT 6 PM MONDAY OCTOBER 25. THE CULVERT WAS CLOGGED WITH MUD, ROCKS AND DEBRIS. NO HOMES WERE IMPACT BUT DRIVEWAYS REQUIRED EXTENSION MUD AND DEBRIS REMOVAL. KRAIL WORKED TO PROTECT PROPERTIES. RAINFALL OF 0.3 TO 0.6 PER HOUR OBSERVED ACROSS YUCAIPA RIDGE.

POTATO CANYON AND OAK GLEN ROADS, POSSIBLE ON BIRCH CREEK, LARGE AMOUNT OF WATER FLOW. VEHICLE WAS SWEPT OFF ROADWAY AND RESCUE WAS PERFORMED. HEAVY RAINFALL OF 0.4 TO 0.6 IN 1 HOUR OCCURRED ACROSS EL DORADO BURN SCAR AND SAN BERNARDINO MOUNTAINS. TOTAL RAINFALL 0.75 TO 1.00 INCHES.
STATE RT 138 BETWEEN CRESTLINE AND PILOT ROCK, SEVERAL ROCK AND MUDSLIDES, SOME ROCKS TO BOWLING BALL SIZE. FLOWING WATER ACROSS ENTIRE ROAD IN SOME LOCATIONS. ROAD IS CURRENTLY PASSABLE.
ROCK AND DEBRIS ON HIGHWAY 2. 2 INCHES OF PRECIPITATION TOTAL ON MT BALDY.
ROCKS IN ROAD ON HIGHWAY 18.
ROCK SLIDE REPORTED IN WATERMAN CANYON HIGHWAY 18.

MUD AND DEBRIS FLOWING ONTO HWY 73 N AND CATALINA VIEW TOLL PLAZA CVT. RAINFALL RATES OF 0.40 TO 0.50 INCHES AN HOUR HAVE OCCURRED OVER THE AREA. AREA HAS RECEIVED 0.80 INCHES OF RAIN IN PAST 6 HOURS.

REPORTS OF ROCKS THE SIZE OF BASKETBALLS SLIDING INTO THE ROAD NEAR CRESTLINE. CROSS STREETS ARE LAKE GREGORY RD AND HORST DR. RAINFALL RATES HAVE BEEN BETWEEN 0.50 AND 0.60 INCHES AN HOUR, WITH 6 HR ACCUMULATION OF JUST UNDER 2 INCHES.

REPORTED 6 DEBRIS FLOWS IN BOND FIRE SCAR AND 4 IN SILVERADO CANYON. SANTIAGO CREEK ROAD BLOCKAGE FROM HIGH WATER FLOWS AND MUD. JACKSON CREEK ROAD FLOODED WITH MUD AND DEBRIS. RAINFALL RATES OF UP TO 1.2 INCHES REPORTED AT IRVINE LAKE AND SANTIAGO CANYON SITE.

PHOTO FROM WEATHER SPOTTER SHARED BY PUBLIC SHOWED 5 FEET OF DEBRIS, MUD AND ROCKS ON SILVERADO CANYON ROAD LOWER PORTION NEAR SANTIAGO CANYON ROAD. ESTIMATED TIME. ROAD WAS COMPLETED COVERED AT THE DEBRIS CROSSING.

VINELAND AND NOBLE IN BEAUMONT AREA. 10+ FOOT LONG TREE BRANCHES/TRUNKS WASHING THROUGH AS WELL AND ROCKS LARGER THAN A FOOT ROUND WASHING THROUGH. WATER FLOWING OVER 1 FOOT OVER CROSSING. WEBCAMERAS SHOWING DARK WATER FLOW AND DEBRIS. APPLE FIRE SCAR. RAINFALL RATES WERE AS HIGH AS 0.60 PER HOUR.
HIGHWAY 38 CLOSED AT BRYANT STREET TO LAKE WILLIAMS DUE TO ROCK AND DEBRIS FLOWS. HEAVY RAIN PAST FEW HOURS.
WEST BOUND SIDE OF LOBELIA DR AT PRIMROSE AVE WAS BLOCKED BY A PILE OF MUD DUE TO A MUDSLIDE.
ROCKS SLIDING INTO ROADWAY ON HWY 18, A FEW MILES SOUTH OF THE OLD WATERMAN CANYON RD/HWY 18 INTERSECTION. AREA HAS RECEIVED ABOUT 0.83 INCHES OF RAIN IN 3 HOURS.
BOULDERS IN ROAD AT THE INTERSECTION OF LONE PINE CANYON RD AND E CANYON NEAR WRIGHTWOOD. AREA HAS RECEIVED CLOSE TO AN INCH OF RAIN IN THE LAST 3 HOURS.
LARGE ROCK IN ROAD JUST SOUTH OF MILE MARKER 18 ON HWY 18 IN THE SOUTH BOUND LANE. AREA HAS RECEIVED AROUND 0.50 INCHES IN LAST 3 HOURS.
ROCK SLIDE REPORTED ON STATE HWY 38 NORTHEAST OF MOUNTAIN HOME VILLAGE. ABOUT A HALF INCH OF RAIN HAS FALLEN IN THIS AREA IN THE PAST 3 HOURS.

REPORT RECEIVED VIA CALTRANS DISTRICT 8 TWITTER ACCOUNT: SR 18 CLOSED IN ARCTIC CIRCLE BETWEEN SNOW VALLEY AND BIG BEAR DAM DUE TO MULTIPLE ROCK SLIDES. ABOUT HALF AN INCH OF RAIN HAS FALLEN OVER THE PAST 3 HOURS.

ROCK AND MUDSLIDE REPORTED. HEAVY RAIN MOVED THROUGH 7 TO 9 AM. HIGHWAY 76 AND EAST GRADE ROAD.
LARGE ROCK IN ROADWAY. HEAVY RAIN JUST MOVED THROUGH AT 830 AM. LA CRESTA BLVD AND MOUNTAIN VIEW RD.

SNOW CREEK MUD AND DEBRIS ON SNOW CREEK ROAD AND HIGHWAY 111 LOW LYING AREA. HAD 1.5 INCHES RAIN PAST 24 HOURS, UP TO 5 INCHES AT NEARBY SNOW CREEK RIDGE. PHOTO SHARED BY CALTRANS.
